evals.prompt: Research what YouTubers are saying about Claude Code and Obsidian — what are the best use cases people are finding?
evals.expected_output: Should search YouTube for Claude Code + Obsidian content, show full search results, auto-select 5-8 relevant videos, create a NotebookLM notebook, add videos as sources, run analysis questions, and present a structured summary with Key Takeaways section
expectations: Runs yt-search script with a query related to Claude Code and Obsidian
Shows the full YouTube search results to the user with metadata
Auto-selects videos without asking the user to pick
Explains which videos were selected and why
Adds selected YouTube URLs as sources
Runs notebooklm ask questions about key themes and takeaways
Presents a summary with a Key Takeaways section
Does NOT ask unnecessary confirmation questions mid-pipeline

evals.prompt: Find YouTube videos about AI coding agents and create a podcast from them
evals.expected_output: Should run the full pipeline through to podcast generation — search, select, notebook, sources, analysis, then trigger notebooklm generate audio and spawn a background agent to wait for completion
expectations: Runs yt-search script with a query about AI coding agents
Auto-selects videos and creates a NotebookLM notebook
Adds videos as sources and waits for processing
Runs analysis questions before generating the podcast
Calls notebooklm generate audio with --json flag
Spawns a background agent to wait for the podcast and download it
Tells the user the podcast is generating and they will be notified

evals.expected_output: Should recognize the query is too vague and ask ONE clarifying question before proceeding. Should NOT just run with 'AI note-taking' as-is or ask multiple follow-up questions.
expectations: Asks exactly one clarifying question to narrow down the topic
Does NOT immediately run the full pipeline without clarification
Does NOT ask more than one question before proceeding
